sections.paragraph:
Isai. 66:7 , usually in the pl. חֲבָלִים, const. חֶבְלֵי m. pains, pangs, especially of parturient woman (see the root in Piel), ὠδῖνες (Syr. ܚܶܒܠܳ id.), Isa 13:8 Jer 13:21. Jer 22:23, בְּבֹא־לָךְ חֲבָלִים “when pangs come upon thee.” Isa 66:7 Hos 13:13. Job 39:3, חֶבְלֵיהֶם תְּשַׁלַּחְנָח properly “they cast forth their pangs,” i.e. they bring forth their young ones with pain. Since the pain of parturition ceases with the birth, a parturient mother may well be said to cast forth her pangs together with her offspring. (In Greek also ὠδὶν is used of offspring brought forth with pain, Eurip. Ion. 45, Æschyl. Agam. 1427). Of other pains once, Job 21:17.

[This word and the following have sometimes been taken as the same; which appears to be correct; see the connection between Act 2:24 and Psa 18:4 ( Heb 5:1-14 Psa 18:5), and 116:3 and see note on the root.]
